zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Ann Stone於1526年出生在英國英格蘭素瑞米爾福德時，她的父親Simon Stone19歲，母親Lady Agnes Clarke19歲。她於1549年與William Roger Trippe在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1605年5月23日在英國英格蘭素福爾可拉文哈姆享年79歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可拉文哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Ann Stone于1526年出生在英国英格兰素瑞米爾福德时，她的父亲Simon Stone19岁，母亲Lady Agnes Clarke19岁。她于1549年与William Roger Trippe在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1605年5月23日在英国英格兰素福爾可拉文哈姆享年79岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可拉文哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .
English: variant of   Stayner  .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Ann Stone was born in 1526, in Milford, Surrey, England, United Kingdom, her father, Simon Stone, was 19 and her mother, Lady Agnes Clarke, was 19. She married William Roger Trippe in 1549, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 23 May 1605, in Lavenham, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 79, and was buried in Lavenham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Ann Stone est née en 1526, à Milford, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Simon Stone, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Clarke, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé William Roger Trippe en 1549, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 23 mai 1605, à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Lavenham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Ann Stoneが1526年にイギリスイギリススリーミルフォードで生まれた時，父Simon Stoneは19歳，母Lady Agnes Clarkeは19歳であった。1549年にイギリススフォルクラベンハムでWilliam Roger Trippeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1605年5月23日にイギリスイギリススフォルクラベンハムで79歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクラベンハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Ann Stone(이)가 1526년 영국 잉글랜드 수리 미퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Simon Stone(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Clarke(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1549년 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에서 William Roger Trippe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1605년 5월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 레이븐함에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 레이븐함에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Ann Stone родилась в 1526 г. в местности милфорд, сурри, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Simon Stone было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Clarke, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Roger Trippe в 1549 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 23 мая 1605 г. в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности лавенам, суффолк, Англия.
